name: getCurrentSkyCultureEnglishName
description: Returns the English name of the currently active sky culture. Sky cultures define how constellations, star names, and celestial artwork are presented based on different cultural traditions (e.g., 'Western', 'Chinese', 'Egyptian').
returnDescription: English name of the current sky culture
example:
var cultureName = StelSkyCultureMgr.getCurrentSkyCultureEnglishName();
core.debug('Current sky culture: ' + cultureName);

name: getCurrentSkyCultureNameI18
description: Returns the translated name of the currently active sky culture in the current UI language. This is the localized display name shown in the GUI.
returnDescription: Translated name of the current sky culture
example:
var localizedName = StelSkyCultureMgr.getCurrentSkyCultureNameI18();
core.debug('Localized culture name: ' + localizedName);

name: setCurrentSkyCultureNameI18
description: Sets the current sky culture using its translated name (as displayed in the GUI). If the name matches a valid sky culture, it will be loaded and applied.

parameters.name: cultureName
parameters.description: Translated name of the sky culture to activate (e.g., 'Western', 'Chinese', 'Egyptian')

returnDescription: true if the sky culture was successfully changed, false otherwise

name: getCurrentSkyCultureID
description: Returns the unique identifier (directory name) of the currently active sky culture. This ID corresponds to the folder name in the skycultures directory.
returnDescription: Sky culture ID (e.g., 'modern', 'chinese', 'egyptian')
example:
var cultureId = StelSkyCultureMgr.getCurrentSkyCultureID();
core.debug('Current culture ID: ' + cultureId);

name: setCurrentSkyCultureID
description: Sets the current sky culture using its unique identifier (directory name). This is the programmatic way to change sky cultures, using IDs like 'modern', 'chinese', or 'egyptian'.
returnDescription: true if the sky culture was successfully changed, false otherwise

name: reloadSkyCulture
description: Reloads the currently active sky culture from disk. Useful after making manual changes to sky culture definition files (index.json, description.md, constellation data, or artwork).

name: getCurrentSkyCultureBoundariesType
description: Returns the type of constellation boundaries used in the current sky culture. Return values: -1=None (no boundaries), 0=IAU (official IAU boundaries), 1=Own (custom boundaries defined by the sky culture).
returnDescription: Boundary type: -1=None, 0=IAU, 1=Own

name: getCurrentSkyCultureClassificationIdx
description: Returns the classification index of the current sky culture. Classifications indicate the nature and origin of the sky culture: 0=Incomplete, 1=Personal, 2=Traditional, 3=Ethnographic, 4=Historical, 5=Single source, 6=Comparative.
returnDescription: Classification index (0-6) as defined in the sky culture index.json

name: getCurrentSkyCultureHtmlClassification
description: Returns an HTML-formatted description of the current sky culture's classification, including the classification name, description, and a color-coded indicator.
returnDescription: HTML string describing the sky culture classification

name: getCurrentSkyCultureHtmlLicense
description: Returns an HTML-formatted description of the license information for the current sky culture, including license type, terms of use, and appropriate color coding (green for free licenses, yellow/red for restricted).
returnDescription: HTML string describing the license terms
example:
var licenseHtml = StelSkyCultureMgr.getCurrentSkyCultureHtmlLicense();
core.output('License information: ' + licenseHtml);

name: getCurrentSkyCultureHtmlRegion
description: Returns an HTML-formatted description of the geographical region of origin for the current sky culture. For the modern sky culture, returns 'World' as the region.
returnDescription: HTML string describing the region of origin
example:
var regionHtml = StelSkyCultureMgr.getCurrentSkyCultureHtmlRegion();
core.debug('Region info: ' + regionHtml);

name: getCurrentSkyCultureHtmlDescription
description: Returns a complete HTML-formatted description of the current sky culture. This includes the culture name, introduction, description sections, constellation details (converted from Markdown), license information, classification, and region information.
returnDescription: Complete HTML description of the sky culture

name: getCurrentSkyCultureNarration
description: Returns a plain text narration of the current sky culture suitable for text-to-speech. This includes the culture name and introduction text (excluding constellation-specific sections, which are narrated individually).
returnDescription: Plain text narration of the sky culture

name: getDefaultSkyCultureID
description: Returns the default sky culture ID as configured in Stellarium's settings. This is the sky culture that will be loaded when the application starts.
returnDescription: Default sky culture ID (e.g., 'modern')
example:
var defaultId = StelSkyCultureMgr.getDefaultSkyCultureID();
core.debug('Default sky culture: ' + defaultId);

name: setDefaultSkyCultureID
description: Sets the default sky culture ID that will be used when Stellarium starts. This setting is saved to the configuration file.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Sky culture identifier to set as default (e.g., 'modern', 'chinese', 'egyptian')

returnDescription: true if the default was successfully set, false if the ID is invalid

name: getSkyCultureListEnglish
description: Returns a list of English names for all available sky cultures. These are the canonical English names used to identify each sky culture.
returnDescription: List of sky culture names in English

name: getSkyCultureListI18
description: Returns a list of translated names for all available sky cultures in the current UI language. These names are suitable for display in GUI menus and dialogs.
returnDescription: List of translated sky culture names

name: getSkyCultureRegionMapI18
description: Returns a map (QMultiMap) where keys are translated sky culture names and values are their associated regions. A single culture may have multiple region entries.
returnDescription: Map of culture names to regions (multiple values per key possible)

name: getSkyCultureTimeLimitMapI18
description: Returns a map where keys are translated sky culture names and values are pairs [beginYear, endYear] indicating the historical time period the sky culture represents. Used for historical sky cultures.
returnDescription: Map of culture names to [beginYear, endYear] time ranges

name: getSkyCultureListIDs
description: Returns a list of all available sky culture identifiers (directory names). These IDs can be used with setCurrentSkyCultureID() to change sky cultures programmatically.
returnDescription: List of sky culture IDs (e.g., ['modern', 'chinese', 'egyptian', ...])

name: getDirToI18Map
description: Returns a map that associates sky culture IDs (directory names) with their translated names in the current UI language. Useful for populating selection dialogs.
returnDescription: Map of culture IDs to translated names

name: getScreenLabelStyleString
description: Returns the current screen label style for celestial objects as a comma-separated string. Styles control how cultural names are displayed on screen (e.g., 'Native', 'Translated', 'IPA', 'Modern', or combinations like 'Native,Translated,IPA').
returnDescription: Screen label style string (e.g., 'Translated', 'Native', 'Native,Translated,IPA')

name: setScreenLabelStyle
description: Sets the screen label style for celestial objects. Controls how cultural names (constellations, stars, planets) are displayed. Styles can be 'none', 'modern', 'byname', 'ipa', 'translated', 'translit', 'pronounce', 'native', or comma-separated combinations like 'Native,Translated,IPA'.

name: getInfoLabelStyleString
description: Returns the current info label style for celestial object information panels as a comma-separated string. This controls how cultural names are displayed in the object info dialog.
returnDescription: Info label style string
example:
var infoStyle = StelSkyCultureMgr.getInfoLabelStyleString();
core.debug('Info label style: ' + infoStyle);

name: setInfoLabelStyle
description: Sets the info label style for celestial object information panels. Controls how cultural names are displayed in the object info dialog. Styles can be 'none', 'modern', 'byname', 'ipa', 'translated', 'translit', 'pronounce', 'native', or comma-separated combinations.

name: getZodiacLabelStyleString
description: Returns the current zodiac label style as a comma-separated string. This controls how zodiac sign names are displayed when the current sky culture defines a zodiac system.
returnDescription: Zodiac label style string

name: setZodiacLabelStyle
description: Sets the zodiac label style for display. Controls how zodiac sign names are shown when the current sky culture defines a zodiac system. Styles can be 'none', 'modern', 'translated', 'native', 'ipa', 'pronounce', 'translit', 'byname', or combinations.

name: getLunarSystemLabelStyleString
description: Returns the current lunar system (lunar mansions/stations) label style as a comma-separated string. Controls how lunar mansion names are displayed when the current sky culture defines a lunar system.
returnDescription: Lunar system label style string

name: setLunarSystemLabelStyle
description: Sets the lunar system (lunar mansions/stations) label style. Controls how lunar mansion names are shown when the current sky culture defines a lunar system. Styles can be 'none', 'modern', 'translated', 'native', 'ipa', 'pronounce', 'translit', 'byname', or combinations.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Lunar system label style: 'none', 'modern', 'translated', 'native', 'ipa', 'pronounce', 'translit', 'byname', or combinations

name: getFlagOverrideUseCommonNames
description: Returns whether the sky culture manager is set to override the sky culture author's 'fallback_to_international_names' setting. When true, common (modern) names are always shown alongside cultural names.
returnDescription: true if common names override is enabled, false otherwise

name: setFlagOverrideUseCommonNames
description: Sets whether to override the sky culture author's 'fallback_to_international_names' setting. When enabled (true), common (modern) names are always shown alongside cultural names regardless of the sky culture's preference.

parameters.name: override
parameters.description: true to always show common names, false to respect sky culture settings

name: getFlagUseAbbreviatedNames
description: Returns whether abbreviated names are used for screen labels. When true, short names (constellation IDs or abbreviations) are displayed instead of full cultural names.
returnDescription: true if abbreviated names are used, false otherwise

name: setFlagUseAbbreviatedNames
description: Sets whether to use abbreviated names for screen labels. When enabled (true), short names (e.g., 'Ori' instead of 'Orion') are displayed. This is useful for decluttered views.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to use abbreviated names, false to use full cultural names

name: currentSkycultureUsesCommonNames
description: Checks whether the current sky culture is configured to incorporate (fall back to) common modern names for celestial objects when cultural names are not available.
returnDescription: true if the sky culture uses common names as fallback, false otherwise

name: getInfoLabelStyle
description:
Returns the current info label style as an integer enum value. This controls how cultural names are displayed in the object info panel. The returned value is a bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags.

Enum values (can be combined):
- 0 = NONE (use modern/common names only)
- 1 = Modern (show modern/IAU names)
- 2 = Byname (show alternative bynames)
- 4 = IPA (show International Phonetic Alphabet pronunciation)
- 8 = Translated (show translated names)
- 16 = Translit (show transliteration)
- 32 = Pronounce (show pronunciation guide)
- 64 = Native (show native/indigenous names)

Common combinations:
- 8 = Translated only
- 64 = Native only
- 72 = Native + Translated (64+8)
- 76 = Native + Translated + IPA (64+8+4)
- 37 = Pronounce + IPA + Modern (32+4+1) - default for many cultures
returnDescription: Integer bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: getScreenLabelStyle
description:
Returns the current screen label style as an integer enum value. This controls how cultural names are displayed on screen (constellations, stars, planets). The returned value is a bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags.

Enum values (can be combined):
- 0 = NONE (use modern/common names only)
- 1 = Modern (show modern/IAU names)
- 2 = Byname (show alternative bynames)
- 4 = IPA (show International Phonetic Alphabet pronunciation)
- 8 = Translated (show translated names)
- 16 = Translit (show transliteration)
- 32 = Pronounce (show pronunciation guide)
- 64 = Native (show native/indigenous names)
returnDescription: Integer bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: getZodiacLabelStyle
description:
Returns the current zodiac label style as an integer enum value. Controls how zodiac sign names are displayed when the current sky culture defines a zodiac system. The returned value is a bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags.

Note: Not all sky cultures have a zodiac system. Use ConstellationMgr.hasZodiac() to check availability.
returnDescription: Integer bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: getLunarSystemLabelStyle
description:
Returns the current lunar system (lunar mansions/stations) label style as an integer enum value. Controls how lunar mansion names are displayed when the current sky culture defines a lunar system. The returned value is a bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags.

Note: Not all sky cultures have a lunar system. Use ConstellationMgr.hasLunarSystem() to check availability.
returnDescription: Integer bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: setScreenLabelStyleEnum
description:
Sets the screen label style using an integer enum bitmask. Controls how cultural names (constellations, stars, planets) are displayed on screen.

Enum values (can be combined with + or |):
- 0 = NONE (modern/common names only)
- 1 = Modern (show modern/IAU names)
- 2 = Byname (show alternative bynames)
- 4 = IPA (show International Phonetic Alphabet pronunciation)
- 8 = Translated (show translated names)
- 16 = Translit (show transliteration)
- 32 = Pronounce (show pronunciation guide)
- 64 = Native (show native/indigenous names)

Common combinations:
- 8 = Translated only
- 64 = Native only
- 72 = Native + Translated
- 76 = Native + Translated + IPA
- 37 = Pronounce + IPA + Modern (default)

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Integer bitmask combining desired CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: setInfoLabelStyleEnum
description:
Sets the info panel label style using an integer enum bitmask. Controls how cultural names are displayed in the object information dialog.

Enum values (can be combined with + or |):
- 0 = NONE (modern/common names only)
- 1 = Modern (show modern/IAU names)
- 2 = Byname (show alternative bynames)
- 4 = IPA (show International Phonetic Alphabet pronunciation)
- 8 = Translated (show translated names)
- 16 = Translit (show transliteration)
- 32 = Pronounce (show pronunciation guide)
- 64 = Native (show native/indigenous names)

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Integer bitmask combining desired CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: setZodiacLabelStyleEnum
description:
Sets the zodiac label style using an integer enum bitmask. Controls how zodiac sign names are displayed when the current sky culture defines a zodiac system.

Enum values (can be combined with + or |):
- 0 = NONE (modern/common names only)
- 1 = Modern (show modern/IAU names)
- 2 = Byname (show alternative bynames)
- 4 = IPA (show International Phonetic Alphabet pronunciation)
- 8 = Translated (show translated names)
- 16 = Translit (show transliteration)
- 32 = Pronounce (show pronunciation guide)
- 64 = Native (show native/indigenous names)

Note: Use ConstellationMgr.hasZodiac() to check if current sky culture has a zodiac system.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Integer bitmask combining desired CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: setLunarSystemLabelStyleEnum
description:
Sets the lunar system (lunar mansions/stations) label style using an integer enum bitmask. Controls how lunar mansion names are displayed when the current sky culture defines a lunar system.

Enum values (can be combined with + or |):
- 0 = NONE (modern/common names only)
- 1 = Modern (show modern/IAU names)
- 2 = Byname (show alternative bynames)
- 4 = IPA (show International Phonetic Alphabet pronunciation)
- 8 = Translated (show translated names)
- 16 = Translit (show transliteration)
- 32 = Pronounce (show pronunciation guide)
- 64 = Native (show native/indigenous names)

Note: Use ConstellationMgr.hasLunarSystem() to check if current sky culture has a lunar system.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Integer bitmask combining desired CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: convertCulturalDisplayStyleFromCSVstring
description:
Converts a comma-separated string of style names to an integer enum bitmask. This is useful for parsing user input or configuration strings.

Valid style names (case-insensitive): 'none', 'modern', 'byname', 'ipa', 'translated', 'translit', 'pronounce', 'native'

Examples:
- 'Translated' → 8
- 'Native,Translated' → 72
- 'Native,Translated,IPA' → 76
- 'Pronounce,IPA,Modern' → 37
returnDescription: Integer bitmask of CulturalDisplayStyle flags

name: convertCulturalDisplayStyleToCSVstring
description:
Converts an integer enum bitmask to a comma-separated string of style names. This is useful for displaying the current style in a human-readable format or saving to configuration.

Valid style names (case-insensitive): 'none', 'modern', 'byname', 'ipa', 'translated', 'translit', 'pronounce', 'native'

Examples:
- 8 → 'Translated'
- 72 → 'Native,Translated'
- 76 → 'Native,Translated,IPA'
- 37 → 'Pronounce,IPA,Modern'
returnDescription: Comma-separated string of style names
